extension.workbenchStudio.command.toggle: Toggle Live Preview (apply settings without reload)
extension.workbenchStudio.enabled.descri: Whether to enable the Workbench Studio patcher.
extension.workbenchStudio.livePreview.de: Poll for settings changes and apply them live without a reload. Off by default. Useful while actively tuning backgrounds/typography; **leave it off for normal use** — polling drives continuous CPU in every window. Turning it **on** requires one reload to start; turning it **off** stops within ~1.5s without a reload.
extension.workbenchStudio.css.descriptio: Raw CSS injected into the workbench. Power-user escape hatch — bypasses typed feature modules and applies the value verbatim inside a managed `<style>` tag. Accepts a string or an array of strings (joined with newlines). **Workspace-aware**; changes require Apply-and-Reload (or enable `workbenchStudio.livePreview`). **Footgun warning**: bad CSS can hide UI or intercept clicks; see [Dangers](https://github.com/webnesto/workbenchStudio/blob/main/docs/dangers.md) for recovery.
extension.workbenchStudio.cssFiles.descr: Path or array of paths to `.css` files. Their contents are read and concatenated with `workbenchStudio.css` before injection. Paths accept `file://` URIs, `~/...` home-relative, absolute, or workspace-relative forms. Changes require Apply-and-Reload (or enable `workbenchStudio.livePreview`). Same footgun caveat as `workbenchStudio.css`.
extension.workbenchStudio.backgrounds.ed: Place the image above or below the code. Can be overridden per-image inside an `images[]` object entry.
Opacity of the editor minimap (scroll preview on the right). Default `0.8` dims it so the background image can show through. `1` keeps minimap fully opaque, `0` hides it.
Seconds of interval for carousel, default `0` to disabled.
extension.workbenchStudio.backgrounds.fu: Opacity of the images, alias to [opacity](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/CSS/opacity). `0.1 ~ 0.3` recommended.
Seconds of interval for carousel, default `0` to disabled.

`true` (default): fullscreen image renders as a translucent overlay on top of the entire workbench (z-index 1000). `false`: image moves behind the workbench (z-index -1). On its own this hides the image — the workbench panes are opaque. To make it visible, transparentify the relevant surfaces via VSCode's `workbench.colorCustomizations` (e.g. `"sideBar.background": "#0000"`, `"editor.background": "#0000"`) or via `workbenchStudio.surfaceOpacity.*`. Footgun — see [Dangers](https://github.com/webnesto/workbenchStudio/blob/main/docs/dangers.md#fullscreen-usefront-false).
extension.workbenchStudio.backgrounds.se: `true` (default): image renders as a translucent overlay above the pane content. `false`: image renders behind. Per-image override supported inside an `images[]` object entry.

CSS [`mix-blend-mode`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/CSS/mix-blend-mode) for this section's background. Empty (default) follows the theme: `screen` on dark themes, none on light. Common values: `normal`, `multiply`, `screen`, `overlay`, `darken`, `lighten`, `hard-light`, `soft-light`. Per-image `mix-blend-mode` inside an `images[]` object entry overrides this for that image.
extension.workbenchStudio.surfaceOpacity: Theme-background opacity per section. `1` keeps the theme color fully opaque (default); `0` makes the section's background transparent so per-section background images can show through. Smart-defaults to `0` when the section has its own background images.
Opacity (0..1) of the editor's theme background color. `0` strips so images show; `1` keeps theme default.
Opacity (0..1) of the sidebar pane's theme background color.
Opacity (0..1) of the panel's theme background color.
Opacity (0..1) of the auxiliary bar's theme background color.
extension.workbenchStudio.typography.exp: Font overrides for sidebar tree views (explorer, source control, search results, extensions, debug). Changes require Apply-and-Reload.
CSS `font-family` value, e.g. `"JetBrains Mono", monospace`. Empty to use the default.
Font size in pixels. `0` to use the default.
CSS `font-weight` value. Empty to use the default.
Freeform CSS passthrough — any property/value pairs (e.g. `letter-spacing`, `line-height`, `color`). Applied after the typed fields, so duplicate keys here override.
extension.workbenchStudio.typography.tab: Font overrides for editor tab labels (the file-name text inside each tab). Changes require Apply-and-Reload. Targets the label only; icons and tab chrome keep their defaults.
CSS `font-family` value, e.g. `"Input Mono", monospace`. Empty to use the default.
Font size in pixels. `0` to use the default.
Freeform CSS passthrough applied to the tab label.
extension.workbenchStudio.typography.pan: Font overrides for pane titles (the 'EXPLORER', 'OUTLINE', etc. headers at the top of each sidebar/panel/auxiliarybar pane) AND composite-bar tabs (e.g. 'CHAT' / 'CLAUDE CODE' view-switcher labels). Changes require Apply-and-Reload.
CSS `font-family` value. Empty to use the default.
Font size in pixels. `0` to use the default.
Freeform CSS passthrough applied to pane titles and composite tabs.
extension.workbenchStudio.urlProtocol.er: The image url must use https or file protocol.
